meta.title: aish — the AI-native shell
meta.description: aish is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers. v0.3 ships a plugin system that installs tools like aish commit as verified prebuilt binaries. Free software, MIT-licensed.

hero.badge: v0.3 shipped — plugins install as prebuilt binaries

subhead.text: is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers. Its plugin system installs tools like
as verified prebuilt binaries — no local build required.

hero.installCaption: Linux & macOS · x86_64 / arm64 · or build from source with cargo.

features.heading: What aish is designed to be

items.title: Extensible Plugins
items.body: A subprocess plugin system: install tools like aish commit with aish plugin install. Binaries are fetched prebuilt from GitHub Releases and verified against SHA256SUMS, falling back to a local cargo build. Plugins run in isolated stdio loops with strict timeouts and bounded stderr.

items.title: Provider-agnostic
items.body: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, and Kilo behind one config and model alias. Never locked to a single vendor — swap models with a flag.

items.title: Open & inspectable
items.body: MIT-licensed free software, shipped as a single static Rust binary. Read the specs, audit the design, send a patch — built in the open from day one.

plugins.heading: Plugin directory
plugins.intro: Official plugins from the aish-plugins registry. Install any with a single command — aish fetches a verified prebuilt binary for your platform.

items.description: AI-generated commit messages from your staged diff.
